summary: Ancient Greek tragedian, author of the Theban Trilogy: Oedipus Rex, Oedipus at Colonus, and Antigone. Also wrote Ajax, Philoctetes, Women of Trachis, and Electra. Most commonly identified by 'call no man happy until he is dead' (quoting Solon) and by Oedipus blinding himself upon discovering he killed his father and married his mother.

works.name: Oedipus Rex (Oedipus the King)
works.description: Sophocles's most famous play. Oedipus investigates a plague and discovers he is the criminal — he killed his father Laius and married his mother Jocasta.

clues.clue: Oedipus discovers he killed his father Laius at a crossroad and married his mother Jocasta; gouges out his eyes upon finding her hanged body
examples: told by the blind seer Tiresias that he caused a plague by killing his predecessor at a crossroad
gouges out his eyes upon finding the hanged body of his wife and mother, Jocasta

clues.clue: Ends with chorus quoting Solon: 'Count no man happy until he is dead'
examples: ends with the chorus quoting Solon's indictment to 'count no man happy' until he is dead
'Call no man happy until he is dead'

clues.clue: Opens with return of embassy to Oracle of Delphi regarding a plague
examples: opens with the return of an embassy to the Oracle of Delphi regarding a plague

clues.clue: Protagonist berates Tiresias for refusing to speak; learns he was abandoned on Mount Cithaeron
examples: berates Tiresias for refusing to speak
learns that he was abandoned on Mount Cithaeron

clues.clue: Sigmund Freud: we are all fated to have sexual impulses toward mothers and murderous impulses toward fathers
examples: Sigmund Freud wrote significantly on the title character, arguing that we are all fated to have sexual impulses toward our mothers

clues.clue: E.R. Dodds wrote an essay on misunderstanding this play inspired by faulty exam responses
examples: Faulty examination responses regarding a play by this author inspired an essay on misunderstanding that play by E.R. Dodds

clues.clue: Jonathan Lear analyzed the 'knowingness' of Oedipus — either/or between reason and religion
examples: Jonathan Lear analyzed the 'knowingness' of one of this author's characters

works.name: Antigone
works.description: Tragedy about Antigone burying her brother Polynices against Creon's orders. She is entombed alive and hangs herself.

clues.clue: Antigone buried alive for sprinkling dust and wine on corpse of her brother Polynices; hangs herself in the cave
examples: later buried alive as a punishment for the burial of her brother Polyneices
title character hangs herself in a cave where she's been left to die after sprinkling dust and wine on the corpse of her brother

clues.clue: Haimon (her fiancé/Creon's son) tries to stab his father, then kills himself
examples: Haimon tries to stab his father before killing himself

clues.clue: Two sisters meet secretly at the opening (Antigone and Ismene)
examples: begins with two sisters meeting secretly

clues.clue: Ode: 'Numberless are the world's wonders, but none more wonderful than man' — birds and beasts 'tamed in the net' of human minds
examples: 'Numberless are the world's wonders, but none / More wonderful than man'

works.name: Oedipus at Colonus
works.description: Final play of the Theban Trilogy. Oedipus dies at Colonus; his tomb in the Grove of the Eumenides protects Athens.

clues.clue: Oedipus's tomb in the Grove of the Eumenides protects Athens; Theseus thwarts a kidnapping; named for an Athenian suburb
examples: the protagonist's tomb in the Grove of the Eumenides protects Athens
Theseus thwarts a kidnapping attempt by a rival ruler

clues.clue: 'The gods alone never die, but Time destroys all other things' — Theseus and Creon fight over the character's grave
examples: states that the gods alone never die, but Time destroys all other things

works.name: Ajax
works.description: Play about Ajax's madness and suicide after losing Achilles's armor to Odysseus.

clues.clue: Athena details how Ajax massacred cattle and sheep in a frenzy; he commits suicide by impaling himself on his sword
examples: Athena details how the title character massacred a herd of cattle and sheep in a frenzy
commits suicide by impaling himself on his sword

clues.clue: Ends with dispute over what to do with Ajax's body — receives proper funeral
examples: ends with a dispute over what to do with the titular character's body after he commits suicide, a debate that ends with him receiving a proper funeral

clues.clue: Title character gives a 'deception speech': 'great, unfathomable time brings dark things into the light'
examples: claims that 'great, unfathomable time / brings dark things into the light' in his climactic 'deception speech'

clues.clue: Chorus from Salamis proclaims allegiance to Ajax
examples: a chorus from Salamis repeatedly proclaims their allegiance to the title character

works.name: Philoctetes
works.description: Play about the archer with a festering snakebite wound on Lemnos, convinced by deified Heracles to return to Troy.

clues.clue: Snakebitten archer on Lemnos; deified Heracles appears as deus ex machina to convince him to go to Troy with Neoptolemus
examples: deified Heracles convinces a character with a festering snakebite wound to go back to Troy with Neoptolemus
wrote about a snakebitten archer in Philoctetes

clues.clue: Restaged as Paradise with all-female cast by Kae Tempest in 2021
examples: restaged under the title Paradise with an all-female cast by Kae Tempest in 2021

works.name: Women of Trachis
works.description: Play about the death of Heracles, accidentally poisoned by Deianeira.

clues.clue: Heracles dying — accidentally poisoned by Deianeira; orders Hyllus to marry Iole on deathbed; prophesied to be killed by someone already dead
examples: dying Heracles, who was accidentally poisoned by Deianeira
Being killed by someone who is already dead is the prophesied fate of one of this playwright's protagonists

clues.clue: Messenger reveals motivations behind assault on Oechalia, contradicting the report of herald Lichas
examples: a messenger reveals the motivations behind another character's assault on Oechalia, contradicting the report of the herald Lichas

works.name: Electra
works.description: Sophocles's version of the Electra story — Orestes and Electra plot to kill Clytemnestra and Aegisthus.

clues.clue: Electra cradles a funeral urn and laments 'No mother did that for you. No nurse. No slave. I.'
examples: laments, 'No mother did that for you. No nurse. No slave. I.' while cradling a funeral urn

clues.clue: Old tutor gives messenger speech claiming Orestes died in a chariot race (deception); Orestes says looking after him is 'rotten work'
examples: an old tutor gives a messenger speech that claims that this person has died in a chariot race
this character says that looking after him is 'rotten work'

clues.clue: Electra argues with sister Chrysothemis; Anne Carson left her cries 'OIMOI MOI' untranslated
examples: cries of 'OIMOI MOI' are left untranslated in Anne Carson's version

comprehensive_summary:
Sophocles was an ancient Greek tragedian, author of the Theban Trilogy (Oedipus Rex, Oedipus at Colonus, Antigone) as well as Ajax, Philoctetes, Women of Trachis, and Electra.

Oedipus Rex is his most famous play. Oedipus investigates a plague in Thebes, is told by the blind seer Tiresias that he himself caused it, and discovers he killed his father Laius at a crossroad and married his mother Jocasta. He gouges out his eyes upon finding her hanged body. The play ends with the chorus quoting Solon: 'Count no man happy until he is dead.' Sigmund Freud drew on the play for his theory of the Oedipus complex. E.R. Dodds wrote about common misunderstandings of the play, and Jonathan Lear analyzed the 'knowingness' of Oedipus.

Antigone depicts the title character burying her brother Polynices against Creon's orders. She sprinkles dust and wine on his corpse, is entombed alive, and hangs herself. Her fiancé Haimon (Creon's son) tries to stab his father and then kills himself. The play contains the famous ode 'Numberless are the world's wonders, but none more wonderful than man.'

In Oedipus at Colonus, the aged Oedipus dies at Colonus, and his tomb in the Grove of the Eumenides protects Athens. Theseus thwarts a kidnapping attempt. Ajax depicts the title hero's madness (Athena reveals he massacred cattle in a frenzy) and his suicide by impaling himself on his sword, followed by a debate over his burial. His 'deception speech' states 'great, unfathomable time brings dark things into the light.'

Philoctetes features a snakebitten archer on Lemnos, convinced by deified Heracles (deus ex machina) to return to Troy with Neoptolemus. Women of Trachis depicts Heracles's death from accidental poisoning by Deianeira; he orders Hyllus to marry Iole on his deathbed. In Electra, the title character cradles a funeral urn and laments 'No mother did that for you. No nurse. No slave. I.' while an old tutor falsely reports Orestes's death in a chariot race.
recursive_suggestions: Oedipus Rex — has its own answerline with many tossups
